source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane/Fire at SP 93 B

actions_taken: Operators kept external fiberglass coverings cool and wet using platform Fire Water Cooling System
Person In Charge shut in 8" Gas Pipeline (Gas Lift) to SP 93 B
Person In Charge shut in 4" Gas and 6" Oil Pipelines from SP 93 B
Emergency distress calls made on VHS Channel 16
Backup Diesel Generator started and put in-service
Fire Water Cooling System Pump started
Emergency Shutdown (ESD) pulled, shutting in platform and all production
M/V Canyon Runner responded with water cannon to extinguish fire
event.summary: On 09-OCT-2015 at 0015 hours, a fire ignited inside the galley/diner on Platform SP 93 B in the Gulf of Mexico. The fire burned for over 8 hours, with at least 4 hours of contained burning with both doors closed. Two operators on the platform detected the fire via alarm and attempted initial containment using the platform's Fire Water Cooling System. The Person In Charge shut in gas and oil pipelines and made emergency distress calls. A nearby vessel (M/V Canyon Runner) responded and assisted in extinguishing the fire using its water cannon. The fire caused extensive structural damage to the galley/diner. Root cause investigation identified improper installation of an electric stove/oven with insufficient clearance from combustible materials in the walls, allowing heat transfer to ignite wood paneling, insulation, and wiring. No personnel injuries occurred.
recommendations: Provide root cause and analysis for fire
Request a Safety Alert concerning proper stove installation for offshore living quarters
USCG request Gulf Land provide letters to their customer base concerning similar installation setups
USCG request that other similar galley buildings be tested for heat transference with a heat gun

hazards.name: Thermal energy from electrical cooking equipment
Confined space with limited fire suppression access

threats.name: Heat transfer from stove through metal walls to combustible materials
Ignition of wood paneling, insulation, and wiring in wall cavities
Uncontrolled fire spread in galley/diner structure

controls.name: Stove installation clearance specification
controls.barrier_role: Prevent heat transfer to combustible materials
controls.lod_basis: Design specification requiring 6 inch clearance at rear and sides from combustible construction

supporting_text: Installation specifications require a 6" clearance at the rear and sides from combustible construction. The stove was secured directly to the rear and right side walls prior to the fire.
Heat transferred from the oven through the metal walls and to combustible wood panels, supports, insulation and wiring.

controls.name: Fire Alarm System
controls.lod_basis: Automatic fire detection system on platform

supporting_text: Both operators were asleep in the overhead sleeping quarters above the galley at the time the Fire Alarm went off.
The two operators only kept the external fiberglass coverings of the buildings cool and wet with water from the platform's Fire Water Cooling System until addition help arrived.
Then they started up the Fire Water Cooling System Pump.

controls.name: Emergency Shutdown (ESD) System
controls.barrier_role: Isolate hazardous materials and shut down production

supporting_text: Then they "Pulled the ESD" shutting in the platform and all production.
the Person In Charge (PIC) at SP 93 A, after being notified, shut in the 8" Gas Pipeline (Gas Lift) to SP 93 B, and the 4" Gas and 6" Oil Pipelines from SP 93 B

controls.name: Management of Change (MOC) approval process
controls.barrier_role: Ensure equipment modifications and new installations are properly reviewed and approved
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory requirement for operations in accordance with approved applications

supporting_text: Review of documents and permits provided to the BSEE Investigator indicated that Energy XXI had not officially requested approval for SP 93 B Platform to utilize and occupy the Gulf Land Rental Buildings, which included: the Galley/Diner and the stacked, Overhead 8 Man Sleeper. The structure has been on location since 2012 and in use for occupancy.
G-115 Authority 802 Enforcement W – Operations Not Conducted in Accordance with Approved Applications.

controls.name: Installation specification verification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure equipment is installed per manufacturer specifications
controls.lod_basis: Manufacturer installation requirements for safe equipment operation

supporting_text: The rental galley was pre-installed with its appliances and equipment before it was delivered offshore by Gulf Land Building Rentals representatives. The installation specification tag is located on the back of the stove, out of view.

The specific ignition source for the fire has been identified by 3rd Party Special Investigators as heat from the electric stove/oven transferred through the metal walls to combustible materials in the walls. Installation specifications require a 6" clearance at the rear and sides from combustible construction. The stove was secured directly to the rear and right side walls prior to the fire.

controls.name: Emergency response and mutual aid
controls.barrier_role: Provide external assistance for fire suppression
controls.lod_basis: Emergency communication and external resource mobilization

supporting_text: He then began making distress calls on VHS Channel 16 to any boats or vessels in the field, to provide assistance of any type to a platform with two personnel on board attempting to contain a galley fire. The M/V Canyon Runner (11 miles away) responded and headed directly to SP 93 B and by 12:50 a.m. arrived and stood by for assistance.
The fire was eventually extinguished once additional help arrived and with the use of the water cannon of the M/V Canyon Runner.

controls.name: Backup power system (Diesel Generator)
controls.barrier_role: Maintain critical systems during power loss
controls.lod_basis: Backup power generation for platform operations

supporting_text: As the operators went to investigate the cause of the alarm, they became aware that the power to the building was off and the emergency lights were on. However, the Gas Generator was still running and the platform production lights were still on.
The operators then started up the back-up Diesel Generator and put it in-service in lieu of the Gas Generator.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
